summary: Verified booking proxy fixes for hourly (timing) integration. Backend forwards raw payload unchanged to C# /api/Auction/addAuction, preserves tripType='timing' (not converted to 'distance'), returns proper 4xx status with structured detail from C#, no longer swallows errors as 'Something went wrong'. 5/5 backend pytest cases pass. Frontend payload construction verified via code review (Playwright cannot fully exercise submit because AuthContext requires real login and account under test has no saved card).

backend_proxy: POST /api/proxy/booking/create with no Authorization -> 401 with {detail: 'Authorization credentials missing'}
POST with malformed JSON body -> 400 {detail: 'Invalid JSON body'}

POST with tripType='timing' + fake Bearer -> C# returns 401, proxy forwards status=401 with structured detail body (e.g. {'detail': {'message': 'Empty response'}}) — NOT the old generic 'Something went wrong' string.
POST with tripType='Transfer' -> proxy still converts to 'distance' server-side (legacy behavior kept). Verified via server-side code (line 775 in csharp_proxy.py) and integration test.
Extra/unknown fields in payload are NOT dropped (Pydantic filtering removed) — payload forwarded unchanged.
verified_behavior.frontend_payload_shape: Code review of /app/frontend/src/pages/HourlyBooking.js lines 123-137 confirms: startPointLatitude/startPointLongitude are Number(pickup.lat/lng), tripType='timing', duration=hours*3600 (seconds), utcOffset=-localDT.getTimezoneOffset() (minutes), paymentType='card', startDate in DD/MM/YYYY HH:mm:ss format. Error handling (lines 149-156) parses body.detail as string or object (message/title/error keys) for hourly-error display.
verified_behavior.frontend_ui: Page /hourly-booking renders correctly: PlacesAutocomplete picks up 'Aéroport de Paris-Charles de Gaulle', 4 car options load (Luxury Sedan 160€, Luxury Van 150€, Standard Car 121€, Standard Minivan 138€), hours selector, date/time inputs, comment, terminal, price=160€ shown. Submit button is disabled when unauthenticated (correct behavior).

critical_code_review_comments: csharp_proxy.py handles empty C# response body (401 with no body) as {'message': 'Empty response'} — this is acceptable but the frontend error display will show the literal 'Empty response' string. Consider mapping 401 from C# to a user-friendly 'Session expired, please sign in again' at the frontend if this scenario is user-facing.
csharp_proxy.py line 803: on connection failure returns {'error':'backend_unreachable', 'message': str(e)} — good, structured detail preserved.

Frontend cannot show hourly-error for full flow validation because the test account (garikgalstyan@gmail.com) has no saved card — main agent should either seed a card or accept curl-level backend validation as sufficient.

success_rate.frontend: verified via code review + UI render

test_credentials: garikgalstyan@gmail.com / 12345678 (has no saved card — full 200 OK submit not exercisable)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Booking proxy fix (raw-body forward + real C# error surfacing) is verified. If future task requires full 200 OK e2e verification, a client account with a saved payment card must be provisioned in C# via /api/Client/addCard flow.
